content:
# John Chapter 17 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_017_page_001.jpg

1. These things the Yeshua spoke, and having lifted up the eyes of him to the heaven, he said, "Father, the hour has come; glorify the Son of you, that the Son may glorify you,

2. just as you gave him authority over all flesh, that all that you have given him, he may give them eternal life.

3. And this is the eternal life, that they may know you, the only true God, and [the one] whom you sent, Yeshua Christos.

4. I glorified you on the earth, having completed the work that you gave me that I should do.

5. And now glorify me, you Father, with yourself, with the glory that I had with you before the world was.

6. I manifested the name of you to the men whom you gave me from the world. Yours they were, and to me you gave them, and the word of you they have kept.

7. Now they have known that all things that you gave me are from you;

8. because the words that you gave me I have given to them, and they received [them] and knew truly that from you I came forth, and they believed that you sent me.

9. I ask concerning them; not concerning the world I ask, but concerning the [ones] whom you gave me, because yours they are.

10. And all the [things] mine are yours, and the [things] yours [are] mine, and I have been glorified in them.

11. And no longer I am in the world, and they are in the world, and I come to you. Holy Father, keep them in the name of you that you have given me, that they may be one just as we [are].

12. When I was with them, I kept them in the name of you that you have given me, and I guarded [them], and none of them perished except the son of the destruction, that the scripture might be fulfilled.

13. But now to you I come, and these things I speak in the world, that they may have the joy of me made full in themselves.

14. I have given them the word of you, and the world hated them, because they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.

15. I do not ask that you take them from the world, but that you keep them from the evil [one].

16. From the world they are not, just as I from the world am not.

17. Sanctify them in the truth; the word of you is truth.

18. Just as you sent me into the world, I also sent them into the world.

19. And for them I sanctify myself, that they also may be sanctified in truth.

20. And not concerning these only I ask, but also concerning the [ones] believing in me through the word of them,

21. that all may be one, just as you, Father, [are] in me and I in you, that they also may be in us, that the world may believe that you sent me.

22. And the glory that you have given me, I have given to them, that they may be one just as we are one:

23. I in them and you in me, that they may be perfected into one, that the world may know that you sent me and loved them just as you loved me.

24. Father, what you have given me, I wish that where I am, they also may be with me, that they may see the glory of me that you gave me, because you loved me before [the] foundation of [the] world.

25. Righteous Father, the world did not know you, but I knew you, and these knew that you sent me.

26. And I made known to them the name of you, and will make [it] known, that the love with which you loved me may be in them, and I in them."

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.3 — "This is eternal life, that they may know you":** Eternal life is defined not as endless duration but as relational knowledge — knowing the only true God and Jesus Christ. γινώσκωσιν (present subjunctive) suggests ongoing, deepening knowledge.

2. **v.5 — "The glory that I had with you before the world was":** πρὸ τοῦ τὸν κόσμον εἶναι — the most explicit pre-existence claim in Jesus' own words. He asks to be restored to a glory he shared with the Father before creation.

3. **v.11 — "Keep them in your name that you have given me":** Sinaiticus reads ᾧ (dative singular, "which") — "the name which you have given me." The Father's name has been given to the Son. This implies shared divine identity — the Son bears the Father's own name (cf. Philippians 2:9-10).

4. **v.12 — "The son of destruction":** ὁ υἱὸς τῆς ἀπωλείας — a Semitic idiom meaning "the one destined for destruction." The same phrase is used of the Antichrist in 2 Thessalonians 2:3. Judas's loss is presented as fulfilling scripture, not as a failure of Jesus' keeping.

5. **v.21 — "That they may all be one":** The prayer for unity is not organizational but ontological — "just as you, Father, are in me and I in you." The unity of believers mirrors the unity of Father and Son. This is the deepest ecumenical text in the NT.

— EMET, AI Translator
